title: System Intent Operating Reality
goal: Implement the fresh #337 lane through the bounded #338 and #339 child slices.
Make surface growth justify itself against the package system intent.
Add a compact effective authority and system-intent embodiment view without creating a heavy workflow layer.
non_goals: Do not implement every existing related issue under #337 in one slice.
Do not close #337 unless the parent lane acceptance is genuinely satisfied.

intent.outcome: Agents and reviewers can cheaply inspect surface-value pressure and effective authority/system-intent embodiment before claiming alignment or closure.
intent.constraints: Keep shipped package behavior repo-, agent-, tool-, language-, and provider-agnostic; prefer existing report/default surfaces before new top-level commands.
intent.latitude: Choose the compact payload shape, test fixtures, and whether #337 remains open after child slices land.
intent.escalation: Stop if implementation becomes a broad scoring system, workflow engine, or historical surface audit.

execution.next_step: Commit and push #339, close it with proof, then keep parent #337 routed open.
execution.proof: Focused workspace tests, contract tooling, summary/reconcile dogfood, planning checker, and ruff for touched Python.

invariants: New durable surfaces must identify repeated cost removed and ownership/authority.
System intent remains directional and compact, not an automatic alignment score.
Roadmap candidates are not execution authority without active planning state.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: close the gap between stated system intent and ordinary package operation

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue remaining #337 child work after #338/#339 or explicitly reroute parent lane

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: ingest newly opened issues, promote the next actionable lane, implement bounded child work end-to-end with package dogfooding, commits, pushes, and closures
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: treat #337 as parent direction; implement #338 then #339 in order
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject additive surface growth unless it reduces repeated future cost and has clear authority/provenance

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ingest and implement the next issues end-to-end
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: stay on master; use checked-in planning; commit/push after milestones; close issues only after proof
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact JSON payload names, tests, and whether parent #337 remains open
delegated_judgment.escalate when: scope expands into broad architecture rewrite or all historical related issues under #337

execution_bounds.allowed paths: workspace package source/tests, planning state/evidence/active execplan, docs only if needed by #338/#339 acceptance
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused pytest for touched workspace behavior; ruff for touched Python; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before creating a new top-level command, broad scoring model, or workflow engine
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated memory implementation, branch state, or broad #230 surfaces

context_budget.live working set: #337 #338 #339, workspace report/default/config/ownership surfaces, contract inventory, system-intent mirror
context_budget.externalize before shift: update execution_run, proof_report, intent_satisfaction, closure_check, and drift_log after each milestone
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace preflight, summary, report, ownership, reconcile
context_budget.tiny resumability note: implement #338 surface-value guardrail then #339 compact authority/system-intent view
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after each commit/push milestone or if adding a new top-level command becomes tempting

stop_conditions.stop when: #338 and #339 are implemented, validated, committed, pushed, and closed, with #337 either kept open or honestly closed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the slice requires a heavy governance process or automatic alignment judgment
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work turns into implementing all #337 related issues or #230
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: summary/checker or focused tests fail after attempted fixes

references.label: system intent operating reality parent lane
effective authority and system-intent embodiment view

immediate_next_action: Add a compact effective authority and system-intent embodiment view.
Reuse existing report/default surfaces and keep unresolved gaps explicit.
invariants: Existing report/default surfaces are preferred over new commands.
Checked-in planning state remains the active authority.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "surface_value or effective_authority or defaults_command or contract"
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
completion_criteria: #338 exposes a compact surface-value guardrail that favors replacement/compression/backgrounding over additive surface growth.
#339 exposes a compact effective authority/system-intent embodiment view through an existing query/report path.
Both child slices have focused tests and dogfood output.
Parent #337 is left open unless its broader related-work acceptance is honestly satisfied.

execution_run.handoff source: preflight, summary, report, ownership, reconcile, live GitHub issue state
execution_run.what happened: Ingested open issues #337, #338, #339, promoted them into active checked-in planning state, implemented #338 surface-value guardrail, and implemented #339 effective-authority/system-intent embodiment view through existing defaults/report surfaces.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI/report/defaults, report schema contract, tests, planning state, external intent evidence, active execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "surface_value or effective_authority or defaults_command or contract"; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section effective_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #338 is closed; #339 is implemented and ready to close after commit/push; parent #337 should remain open for broader acceptance.
execution_run.next step: Commit and push #339, close it, then archive this child-slice plan while keeping #337 routed.

finished_run_review.intent served: child slices satisfied; parent remains broader
finished_run_review.config compliance: used compact package surfaces and avoided new top-level commands

proof_report.validation proof: Focused workspace CLI/contract tests, ruff, contract tooling checker, planning checker, effective-authority defaults/report dogfood, summary dogfood, and git diff whitespace check passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: execution_run.validations run lists the passing commands

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Ingest and implement the next planned issue work end-to-end.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Stay quiet, repo-native, low-residue, and sharply bounded while reducing total continuation, handoff, review, and proof cost.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Add compact queryable guardrails only where they reduce repeated future cost.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make alignment and authority cheaper to inspect without adding framework feel?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #338 and #339 are implemented, but parent #337 includes broader follow-on work that is not fully satisfied.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: surface_value_guardrail and effective_authority defaults/report payloads, schema contracts, tests, and dogfood output
closure_check.reopen trigger: agents again lack a compact answer for surface value pressure, effective authority, current work owner, or unresolved system-intent gaps

execution_summary.outcome delivered: #338 surface-sprawl guardrail and #339 compact effective-authority/system-intent embodiment view implemented in existing defaults/report surfaces.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused CLI/contract tests, ruff, contract tooling checker, planning checker, defaults/report/summary dogfood output, and diff check passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: #337 remains open for broader system-intent operating-reality follow-through.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The new checks live in workspace defaults/report payloads and schema contracts rather than new first-line documents.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Reingested open issues and promoted #337/#338/#339 into active planning state.
2026-04-26: Implemented #338 surface-value guardrail through existing defaults/report surfaces.
2026-04-26: Implemented #339 effective-authority/system-intent embodiment view through existing defaults/report surfaces.
